posts.title: PVHS Weekly Newsletter
posts.summary: WEEKLY UPDATE PIONEER VALLEY HIGH SCHOOL May 1, 2026 Hello Panthers Welcome to this week's edition of the Pioneer Valley Newsletter! We're excited to share the latest happenings...
posts.author: Araceli Mandujano-Padilla
posts.body:
WEEKLY UPDATE

PIONEER VALLEY HIGH SCHOOL

May 1, 2026

Hello Panthers

Welcome to this week's edition of the Pioneer Valley Newsletter! We're excited to share the latest happenings and updates from our school community to keep you informed and engaged.
 

Paul R. Robinson, Ph.D. - Principal


Tiger Salamander Field Trip
Mr. Magni, Ms. Mihelic and Mrs. Zambrano took students on a field trip to a Tiger Salamander breeding facility. Students viewed the habitats and learned how scientists are helping support this sensitive population. Thank you teachers for providing such a wonderful educational experience for our students.
 
Superintendent Visit
Superintendent Garcia visited PVHS this week to meet with our Student Advisory Council. Mr. Garcia gathered input from our students on a possible update to our District cell phone policy. California's Assembly Bill 3216, the Phone-Free School Act, requires Districts to have a policy in place by June 2026. SMJUHSD currently has an “off and away” during instructional time policy, with the discretion for teachers to allow phones as needed for instruction. Mr. Garcia gathered input for an update to this policy and students discussed advantages and disadvantages of cell phone use on campus.
 
MWH DRS
Modern World History teachers engaged in a Data Reflection Session this week to discuss Cycle 4 test results. This engagement with student data helps teachers identify and expand our best practices. Thank you teachers for your hard work identifying how to best serve our students.

AVID 2027 Service Day
Ms. Wisz's AVID class hosted their community service project, Spring Family Fun Day, at Waller Park on Saturday. The event featured a variety of family friendly activities, including an Easter egg hunt, face painting, sponge and stencil painting, construction paper crafts, bracelet making, freeze tag, and soccer.
Snacks that the students and kids enjoyed were provided, and the event drew a strong turnout, allowing AVID students to offer kids and families a fun, no cost experience.
 

Merrie Okie-Goldin - Assistant Principal, Special Programs

Hello Panthers,
Riccardo Magni, Pioneer Valley High School’s AP Environmental Science teacher and Science Department Chair, was honored at the 2026 Earth Day celebration last Saturday at the Natural History Museum in Santa Maria for his decades of dedication to environmental education. With 30 years of teaching experience, Magni is known for his hands-on, field-based approach that students to engage deeply with the natural world. A recipient of multiple honors, including the Presidential Innovation Award and County Teacher of the Year. His impact extends beyond the classroom, as many of his students have gone on to pursue environmental careers and advocacy. Congratulation Riccardo!

Also, CAASPP testing will continue into next week for any students who have not yet completed their exams. Students who still need to finish will be called out of class to complete their testing. If a teacher needs to keep a student, we will coordinate to call them during another class period that allows for testing. Our goal is to reach 100% participation to meet state requirements, so we truly appreciate your support in reminding your student to come to school each day with a fully charged tablet.

We also want to extend a heartfelt thank you to our incredible staff for the dedication and effort poured into testing. A special thank you to our Department Chairs, TOSA's, ELPAC teachers, Math, Science, and English teachers for leading this work so diligently, and to team members Sasha Brafman, Jordyn MacCuish, Julie Swayze, Erica Dutton, Dawnette Cress and Amber Bernard for their support behind the scenes. We’re also grateful to Karine Perez, and to Cecilia Badley for coordinating substitutes and testing rooms. A special thank you to our community liaison, Flor Avila-Suarez, for stepping in alongside Special Education Secretary, Jessica Munoz, to help organize and call-in students who have been absent. Thank you to our Attendance team. Ben Alberry and Izzy Hernandez, you are smart cookies too! Thank you to Jenn Montanez and Scratch Cafe for the thank you treats for staff. Thank you to parents, guardians, and students for your attendance and support with participation.

This truly takes up a village, and we appreciate our admin team and admin assistants and everyone who has stepped up to support this big push. Thank you all for your help!
 

Jeffrey Hromadka - Assistant Principal, Student Services

🌟 A Massive Success at Future Panther Night!
What an incredible evening! This past Wednesday, the Panther Gym was buzzing with energy as we welcomed a huge crowd of prospective students and families for our Future Panther Night. The turnout was a testament to the vibrant community we are building here at Pioneer Valley High School.

🎭 Center Stage: Showcasing Our Talent
The night kicked off with a series of spectacular performances that showcased the diverse talent on our campus. We were treated to a captivating sneak peek of "Fiddler on the Roof" by our Drama department, vibrant cultural displays from Ballet Folklórico, and high-energy routines from the K-Pop Club. The FFA highlighted our strong agricultural roots, while the PVHS Drum Line provided an electrifying beat that kept the energy high all night long!

🤝 Finding Your "Crew"
After the performances, the floor transformed into a bustling marketplace of opportunity. Dozens of clubs and sports teams were on hand to meet with students.
We know that one of the greatest barometers of success for a high school student is being part of a group. Whether it’s on a field, in a lab, or on a stage, finding a sense of belonging is the first step toward a fulfilling four-year journey. Seeing so many future Panthers "shopping" for their extracurricular home was the highlight of the event!

❤️ A Heartfelt Thank You
This event would not have been possible without the dedication of our staff. Thank you to the teachers and coaches who stayed late to represent your programs. Your passion is infectious, and your encouragement is what gives these incoming students the confidence to take that first step toward success.
To our future Panthers: the energy you brought on Wednesday was amazing. We can’t wait to see you back on campus this fall!

 

Kevin Ilac - Assistant Principal, Instruction

This week was Sexual Assault Awareness Week. The Wellness Center, in partnership with the SHARe & Care Club, hosted multiple awareness activities during lunch. These activities helped raise awareness, promote consent, and remind our school community that support and resources are available.
  
On Thursday, the C & I Team held an AHC Registration Rally. Over 130 seniors completed the last step of their Allan Hancock Promise and registered for their Fall classes. Students that completed all of their steps were awarded priority registration at 9:00 am on Thursday. 
 
  Friday, 78 seniors who committed to a four-year university were celebrated with a signing day ceremony at lunch. May 1st is traditionally known as "Signing Day" in the United States. Students were recognized for their amazing achievement in the amphitheater. 
 

Christian Basulto - Dean of Students

Panther of the Quarter
This Tuesday, we proudly celebrated our Panther of the Quarter winners. Students and staff who have gone above and beyond to demonstrate the values of Panther PRIDE were recognized. The event was a huge success and a true reflection of the positive energy on our campus. A big thank you goes out to Mr. Dickinson’s Floral students for creating the beautiful flower arrangements and decorating the tables, which added such a warm and elegant touch to the celebration. 

Congratulations to our student winners: (9th grade) Julissa Alvarenga Torres, Frankie Hernandez, Danna Jacobo Rivera, and Jordan Nunez Nevarez; (10th grade) Brenda Antonio Santos, Brigida Calleja Mentado, Camille Gutierrez, and Alex Vargas-Vasquez; (11th grade) Ethyn Enriquez, Aubrey Johnson, Kiera Leon, and Luis Reyes-Velasco; and (12th grade) Jeffrey "JJ" Brown, Angel Daniel Camacho, Kevin Montes, and Paula Novela Rodrigeuz. These students were nominated for consistently showing leadership, responsibility, and care for others both inside and outside the classroom.

We also honored four outstanding staff members for their hard work and dedication. Congratulations to certificated staff Allison Pehle and Regina Orosco, and classified staff Andres Cardenas and Leticia Gamino! Thank you to everyone who helped us recognize the amazing people who help make PVHS such a special place.
 
PVHS Boys' Volleyball
This Thursday, our PVHS Boys Volleyball team competed against Mission Prep for the Ocean League Championship, and they delivered! The Panthers battled hard and came away with a 3-1 victory to secure the league title.
The fans in attendance were in for a treat. Set scores were 25-17, 27-25, 22-25, and the team closed out the match with an exciting 30-28 win in the fourth set!! 
Congratulations to our players and coaches on an incredible season and well-earned league championship. Best of luck as they move on to compete in the CIF playoffs!
Go Panthers! 🐾🏐

 
 
PVHS WEBSITE

posts.title: Final Reminder: Future Panther Night is Tomorrow (4/29 Wednesday)
posts.summary: FINAL REMINDER: Future Panther Night is TOMORROW (Wed 4/29)! (Note Time Change)We are officially less than 24 hours away from welcoming our newest Panthers! Please take note of...
posts.author: Jeffrey Hromadka
posts.body:
FINAL REMINDER: Future Panther Night is TOMORROW (Wed 4/29)! (Note Time Change)

We are officially less than 24 hours away from welcoming our newest Panthers! Please take note of a slight update to our schedule to ensure you don’t miss our exciting opening ceremonies.

⏰ Updated Schedule:

  • 5:30 PM: Doors Open (Panther Gym)
  • 5:45 PM: Live Performances Begin!
Make sure to arrive by 5:30 PM to grab a seat before our student performers take the floor to kick off the night with a bang!

🎒 What’s in Store for You?

Once the performances conclude, the floor is yours! This is the ultimate "one-stop shop" for everything Pioneer Valley:
  • Club & Team Showcase: From the soccer field to the robotics lab, come "shop" for the activities that make high school memorable.
  • Meet Your Coaches & Teachers: Get face-to-face time with the mentors who will support your student over the next four years.
  • Essential Info: Get the final word on what you need for a successful transition this fall.

📍 Location Recap

Where: The Panther Gym
When: Wednesday, April 29th @ 5:30 PM- 7:00PM
We’ve put in the work to make this a fun, engaging, and informative night for the whole family. We can't wait to see the gym filled with the energy of our future Class of 2030!
See you tomorrow night! Go Panthers! 🐾

posts.title: 📢 CAASPP Testing Begins Tomorrow – Juniors (ELA & Math)
posts.summary: 📢 CAASPP Testing Begins Tomorrow – Juniors (ELA & Math)Dear Students, Staff, and Families,Tomorrow, our junior students will begin the California Assessment of Student Performa...
posts.author: Merrie Okie
posts.body:
📢 CAASPP Testing Begins Tomorrow – Juniors (ELA & Math)
Dear Students, Staff, and Families,
Tomorrow, our junior students will begin the California Assessment of Student Performance and Progress (CAASPP) testing in English Language Arts and Mathematics.
The CAASPP system includes the Smarter Balanced Assessments (SBAC), which measure what students know and are able to do based on California’s academic standards in reading, writing, and math. These assessments are an important statewide requirement and provide valuable information about student learning, progress, and readiness for college and careers.
This is a state-mandated assessment, and it is critical that we have all students present and participating so we meet the required participation rate.
✅ What we need from families:
  • Please ensure your student is present, on time, and ready to test
  • Encourage a good night’s sleep and a charged device 
  • Reinforce the importance of doing their best
Our goal is to achieve 100% participation, and your support makes a big difference.
Testing will continue over the next couple of weeks, so consistent attendance is essential during this time.
If you have any questions, please feel free to reach out via email or phone call.
Thank you for your partnership and support!

Merrie Goldin 
Assistant Principal over Special Programs
805-922-1305 Ext. 5705

posts.title: Future Panther Night
posts.summary: ⚡ Don't Miss Out: Discover the Panther Experience!We are just days away! If you are a prospective student or parent that has a student joining Pioneer Valley High School family...
posts.author: Jeffrey Hromadka
posts.body:
⚡ Don't Miss Out: Discover the Panther Experience!

We are just days away! If you are a prospective student or parent that has a student joining Pioneer Valley High School family this fall, our Future Panther Night is the one event you don't want to miss.

📍 When & Where

• Wednesday, April 29th

• 5:00 PM – 7:00 PM

• Location: The Panther Gym

🎨 Beyond the Classroom: Shop Your Future!

High school is about more than just textbooks—it’s about finding your passion and your "crew." We’ve designed this night to be interactive and engaging for students:

• Club & Sports Fair: Students can "shop" through dozens of extracurricular activities. Whether it's joining a sports team or finding a niche hobby in a student club, this is where the fun begins.

• Talk to the Pros: Coaches and teachers will be available at their booths to chat one-on-one about what makes PVHS such an exciting place to learn and grow.

• Ask a Panther: Get the inside scoop from current staff and leaders about how to make the most of the next four years.

📚 Parent Peace of Mind

While the students explore, parents will receive all the essential information needed to ensure a smooth transition in August. We’ll cover everything from campus culture to the resources available to help your student thrive.

Come see what makes being a Panther so special. We can’t wait to welcome the Class of 2030!

posts.title: Sexual Assault Awareness Week | Wellness Center Activities & Resources
posts.summary: The Wellness Center, in partnership with the SHARe & Care Club, is hosting Sexual Assault Awareness Week this week. This is a time to raise awareness, promote consent, and remin...
posts.author: Crystal Buchan
posts.body:
The Wellness Center, in partnership with the SHARe & Care Club, is hosting Sexual Assault Awareness Week this week. This is a time to raise awareness, promote consent, and remind our school community that support and resources are available.
Please see the attached flyer for our daily dress‑up themes and on‑campus activities, including:

Monday (4/27): Wear Teal, the color for Sexual Assault Awareness
- Information tabling at lunch in front of the Wellness Center
Tuesday (4/28): Consent is Black & White- wear Black & White
Wednesday (4/29): Denim Day Rally
- Tabling at the Amphitheater with a K‑POP performance and the North County Rape Crisis Center
Thursday (4/30): Support Survivors Day
- Sign the banner in the Wellness Center with messages of encouragement
Friday (5/1): Wear Flannel
- Highlighting that anyone can be affected by sexual assault, regardless of background or appearance

Throughout the week, students are welcome to visit the Wellness Center to participate in activities, ask questions, or speak privately with a trusted adult.

Support & Resources
If you or someone you know needs support, help is available:

Local Resources:
North County Rape Crisis & Child Protection Center (serving Santa Maria & Santa Barbara County)
24‑Hour Hotline: (805) 564‑3696
https://www.ncrcc.org<https://www.ncrcc.org/>

National Resources:
National Sexual Assault Hotline (RAINN): 800‑656‑HOPE (4673)
https://www.rainn.org<https://www.rainn.org/>
(Confidential support via phone or chat, 24/7)
988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline: Call or text 988 for emotional support, 24/7

Participation is optional, and all students are encouraged to engage at a level that feels comfortable for them. We appreciate your support in helping create a safe, respectful, and caring school environment.

If you have questions or would like additional support, please visit or contact Mrs. Buchan, Wellness Counselor or Ms. Collins, Certified Wellness Coach.

posts.title: Save the Date (Wed, April 29 from 5:00-7:00) Future Panther Night!
posts.summary: 🐾 Save the Date: Future Panther Night!Calling all prospective students and families! Are you ready to join the Pioneer Valley High School community? We are thrilled to invite y...
posts.author: Jeffrey Hromadka
posts.body:
🐾 Save the Date: Future Panther Night!

Calling all prospective students and families! Are you ready to join the Pioneer Valley High School community? We are thrilled to invite you to our Future Panther Night—the perfect opportunity to get a head start on your high school journey.

🗓️ Event Details

  • When: Wednesday, April 29th
  • Time: 5:00 PM – 7:00 PM
  • Where: Pioneer Valley High School Gym (The Panther Gym)

🔍 What to Expect

This evening is designed to provide you and your student with everything you need to know for the upcoming fall semester. It's more than just a meeting; it’s a chance to see why it’s a great day to be a Panther!
  • Academics & Info: Get essential details regarding registration, curriculum, and what to expect during your first year at PVHS.
  • Club & Sports "Shopping": Explore our various extracurricular activities! We will have booths set up where students can browse different clubs and sports teams.
  • Meet the Team: Coaches and teachers will be on hand to answer questions, share their passion, and help students find ways to make high school fun and engaging.
  • Get Involved: Learn about the programs that build school spirit and create lifelong memories.
Whether you're interested in the arts, athletics, or academic clubs, there is something for everyone. We can’t wait to meet our newest class of Panthers and help you get plugged into our campus culture.
We look forward to seeing you there!

posts.title: PVHS Weekly Newsletter 4/24/2026
posts.summary: WEEKLY UPDATE PIONEER VALLEY HIGH SCHOOL April 24, 2026 Hello Panthers Welcome to this week's edition of the Pioneer Valley Newsletter! We're excited to share the latest happeni...
posts.author: Araceli Mandujano-Padilla
posts.body:
WEEKLY UPDATE

PIONEER VALLEY HIGH SCHOOL

April 24, 2026

Hello Panthers

Welcome to this week's edition of the Pioneer Valley Newsletter! We're excited to share the latest happenings and updates from our school community to keep you informed and engaged.
 

Paul R. Robinson, Ph.D. - Principal

Taco Tuesday
PVHS Staff gathered to enjoy tacos presented by the District Administration. This was a great time for staff to gather and enjoy time with colleagues they don’t see often and to meet and great with the District Administration.
 
Hiring Update
PVHS is happy to report that we have hired 2 International Language teachers and four Special Education teachers. We are currently working to fill our Instructional Coach positions for English and Multilingual. All classroom positions are fully staffed.

Riccardo Magni to be honored Saturday at Natural History Museum event
Riccardo Magni is one of two teachers being recognized this Saturday for outstanding research in environmental studies. Please join us in congratulating Mr. Magni on his hard work, dedication, and commitment to excellence.

Click here for full article: Earth Day Conservation Award winners to be honored Saturday at Natural History Museum event | Local News | santamariatimes.com
 

Merrie Okie-Goldin - Assistant Principal, Special Programs

Hello Panthers!

Pioneer Valley High School is excited to host our annual districtwide Special Education Prom, An Adventure with SpongeBob! This joyful celebration brings together students from all of our district high schools for a memorable morning of fun, friendship, and community. Hosted by our Student Council and Special Education Department, the prom will be held in the gym from 9:15–11:30 a.m. and will feature music, dancing, decorations, treats, and so much more.

Students will be dressed up and ready to celebrate with activities including face painting, friendship bracelets, kinetic sand, and rock painting. This special event is a wonderful tradition that highlights inclusion, connection, and the spirit of celebration shared by our students, teachers, instructional assistants, and staff.

A heartfelt thank you to all of our staff and families who support this event, and special thanks to Lisa Walters, Activities Director,and our Special Education departments for helping make this amazing experience possible. We are proud to continue this beautiful Santa Maria district tradition!

🧪 CAASPP Testing Update
CAASPP testing is currently underway, and next week we will be testing in English & Math. Testing will continue on Tuesday, Wednesday, and Thursday, and Friday of next week.
Thank you to our students, staff, and families for your continued support, preparation, and flexibility as we move through this important state mandated assessment period.

Call with any questions-
Merrie Goldin Ext. 5707

 

Jeffrey Hromadka - Assistant Principal, Student Services

Senior Spotlight: Camila Echeverria Signs!
A huge round of applause for senior student-athlete Camila Echeverria (Women’s Soccer)! On Wednesday, April 22nd, we gathered in the amphitheater to celebrate Camila as she officially committed to continue her playing career at Park University in Gilbert, AZ.
Camila has been a cornerstone of our soccer program, and we couldn’t be prouder to see her take her talents to the next level. Congratulations, Camila—the Buccaneer family is rooting for you!

📈 Postseason Fever: 5 Teams Headed to CIF!
The hard work during the regular season has officially paid off. We are thrilled to announce that five of our spring teams have secured their spots in the CIF Playoffs!
Please join us in congratulating the players and coaches of the following programs: Boys Baseball, Girls Softball, Boys Volleyball, Boys Tennis, and Track & Field.
Good luck in the post season!

 

Kevin Ilac - Assistant Principal, Instruction

Today is the last day of the 2nd progress period for Spring. Progress grades will be sent to parents next week via ParentSquare. This means there are only 6 weeks left of school which ends June 10th.

This week PV counselors and AVID teachers administered the 10th grade PSAT to 90 eager Sophomores. This exam helps prepare students for future Advanced Placement and college entrance exams. 

On Tuesday PVHS hosted Allan Hancock College counselors for our second Launch for College event. Hancock counselors were able to service over 200 seniors with their transition plans to junior college next year.  (PICS INCLUDED BELOW)

Senior students going to a 4-year university next year need to make sure they inform Mrs. Mederos or Mr. Hernandez which university they will be attending next year in order to participate in the signing ceremony next Friday May 1st at lunch. 

 

Christian Basulto - Dean of Students

Glow in the Dark FFA Meeting
Last Thursday, April 16, our PVHS FFA program hosted their April meeting in the gym with a fun and energetic “Neon Nights” theme. The meeting kicked off with our FFA leadership students reviewing past events and sharing updates on exciting upcoming activities. Attendees also had the opportunity to hear from students running for chapter officer positions, as each candidate introduced themselves and shared why they are interested in serving in a leadership role. After the meeting portion, students enjoyed glow-in-the-dark volleyball, dodgeball, and badminton, creating a lively and memorable atmosphere. The night wrapped up with pizza, giving students time to connect and celebrate together. It was a great event that truly highlighted the energy, leadership, and school spirit of our FFA program. A huge thank you to our students and staff for putting together such a fantastic FFA meeting and for all of the hard work they have put in throughout the year—we are proud of you!
 
Emily Cipriano-FFA Speaking Competition Recognition
On that same day, April 16, we are proud to celebrate Emily Cipriano, who traveled to Fresno State to compete in the State FFA Speaking Competition. Emily qualified to compete in the Spanish Creed (El Credo) contest at the state level and earned an impressive 3rd High Individual in the entire state. This is an outstanding accomplishment and a true reflection of her dedication, hard work, and commitment to excellence. Her success was proudly announced at the FFA meeting, where fellow students shared in the excitement and celebrated her achievement. Congratulations, Emily, on representing PVHS in such an incredible way!
 
Final ELAC Meeting
This week, we held our final ELAC meeting of the school year on Wednesday, April 22 at 6:00 PM in the PVHS Library. It was a strong way to close out the year, featuring three informative presentations for our families. Sergio Magdalenogarcia, RN presented on tuberculosis, Mayra Luis Antonio, Santa Barbara County Health Educator, shared important information about vaccines, and Elia Rodriguez, Associate Director, provided an overview of CenCal services. These presentations helped keep our families informed on important health topics that impact our community.

Thank you to all of our parents who attended and engaged in the conversation. A special thank you to our staff, including Karine Perez, our guidance techs, community liaison, interpreters, and Dr. Robinson, for their continued support in making these meetings a success. We had a fantastic year building connections and strengthening relationships with our families, and we look forward to continuing this important work in next year’s ELAC meetings!

 
 
PVHS WEBSITE
